_metadata.title: SGA × SAP HANA Integration Master Plan
_metadata.author: SGA Development Team

OITB.description: Item Groups (ASHLAND, BASF, IFF, LUBRIZOL, etc.)

OIBT.description: Item Batch Stock per Warehouse (live quantities)

RDR1.description: Sales Order Lines (with U_KilosPre, U_Tara, U_NumEtiqueta, U_Presentacion)

OWOR.description: Production Order Headers (672 orders, includes StartDate, DueDate, Status)

OBIN.description: Bin Locations (shelving A-01 through A-14, etc.)

ATC1.description: Document Attachments (SDS, COA PDFs linked to items)

U_Peligro.purpose: H-Statements (Hazard phrases, comma-separated codes or full text)

U_Pictograma1.purpose: GHS Pictogram 1 code (G=flame, H=health, I=environment, E=corrosion, etc.)

U_NumEtiqueta.purpose: Label number/count per sales order line

implemented.description: Load a SAP sales order by DocNum, enrich items with batch/tare data, return to frontend for label queue
Fetch Fecha de Fabricación (MnfDate) and Fecha de Vencimiento (ExpDate) from batch master per item
If tare weight not in local CSV, query SAP OITM BWeight1/BWeight2 (gross/tare)

implemented.feature: SAP Order Import to Local DB
implemented.description: Import single SAP order into local order status tracking database
Bulk import/sync recent orders and their SAP status (Abierto/Cerrado/Cancelado)
Lightweight sync of 50 open orders for TV dashboard display
Manual SAP HANA connection test and reconnect from dashboard

not_integrated: Product master sync from SAP OITM
GHS UDF data from OITM (U_Peligro, U_Prudencia, U_Word, U_Pictograma1-4)
Sales order line UDFs (U_Tara, U_NumEtiqueta, U_Presentacion, U_KilosPre)

phase_1_quick_wins.title: Phase 1 — Quick Wins (1-2 weeks)
phase_1_quick_wins.description: Leverage data already in SAP to immediately improve label accuracy and reduce manual work

features.name: GHS Data Sync from SAP UDFs
features.description: Read U_Word, U_Peligro, U_Prudencia, U_Pictograma1-4 from SAP OITM and use as primary GHS data source, falling back to local unified_db CSVs only when SAP data is missing
features.impact: HIGH — 388 items already have GHS data in SAP. Eliminates need to maintain separate CSV for these items
implementation: Add get_item_ghs_data(item_code) method to sap_connector.py
In SmartLabelManager, try SAP UDFs first, fallback to CSV
Map SAP pictogram codes (G/H/I/E/etc.) to pictogram filenames
Handle 'No Aplicable' signal words (skip label generation for non-hazardous)
risks: SAP UDF data may have encoding issues (accented chars truncated in NCLOB)
Pictogram code mapping needs verification (single-letter codes to PNG filenames)

features.name: Sales Order Line UDFs for Labels
features.description: Read U_Tara (tare weight), U_NumEtiqueta (label count), U_Presentacion (packaging type), and U_KilosPre (pre-calculated kg) directly from RDR1 sales order lines
features.impact: HIGH — Eliminates guessing tare weight from CSV lookup. SAP already has the correct tare per order line
implementation: Extend get_order_details() query to include U_Tara, U_NumEtiqueta, U_Presentacion, U_KilosPre from RDR1
In /labels/sap/load, use U_Tara directly instead of CSV lookup + SAP fallback
Use U_NumEtiqueta as default copy count
Display U_Presentacion in label queue UI
risks: U_Tara may be stored as string, needs parsing

features.name: Real-Time Batch Stock per Warehouse
features.description: Show which batches are available in which warehouse with live quantities, so operators select the correct batch when generating labels
features.impact: HIGH — Operators currently guess batch numbers. This shows exactly which batches exist in each warehouse with FeFa/FeVe dates
implementation: Add get_item_batches(item_code, warehouse=None) to sap_connector.py joining OIBT + OBTN
Add API endpoint GET /api/sap/batches/<item_code>?warehouse=01
Add batch picker dropdown in label queue UI (replaces free-text batch input)
Auto-populate FeFa/FeVe when batch is selected
risks: Query performance for items with many batches

features.name: Product Master Sync (SAP → Local DB)
features.description: Bulk sync product master data from SAP OITM to local unified_db CSVs. This ensures new products added in SAP are automatically available for labeling
features.impact: MEDIUM — Currently 1,151 SAP items but local CSV may not have all. Sync eliminates manual product entry
implementation: Add sync_products_from_sap() endpoint that queries OITM + OITB
Map SAP fields to products_master.csv columns
Merge with existing CSV (don't overwrite GHS data from local DB)
Add 'Last SAP Sync' timestamp to dashboard
Support incremental sync via UpdateDate filter
risks: Must not overwrite local GHS CSV data that SAP doesn't have

phase_2_core_integration.title: Phase 2 — Core Business Integration (2-4 weeks)
phase_2_core_integration.description: Deeply connect SGA with SAP business processes for inventory, deliveries, and production

features.name: Real-Time Inventory Dashboard
features.description: Live stock levels per item per warehouse from SAP, with batch breakdown. Shows total available, committed, and on-order quantities
features.impact: HIGH — Warehouse operators can see stock without opening SAP client
implementation: Add get_stock_levels(item_code=None, warehouse=None) to sap_connector.py
New route /inventory/ with warehouse selector
Show stock cards: OnHand from OITM, per-warehouse from OITW, per-batch from OIBT
Color-code low stock items (below MinLevel from OITM)
Add stock summary to dashboard widgets

features.name: Delivery Note Integration
features.description: When labels are printed for a SAP order, auto-create or link to the delivery note. Show which batches were assigned to each delivery
features.impact: HIGH — Links label printing to the shipping process. Proves batch traceability from label → delivery → customer
implementation: Add get_delivery_notes(order_number) to sap_connector.py via OriginNum link
Show delivery status in order detail page
Display batch allocations per delivery (from IBT1 WHERE BaseType=15)
Track which batches were labeled vs delivered
Optionally write back label-print event as delivery note comment
risks: SAP DI API required for creating documents (read-only via SQL is safe, writing requires SAP Service Layer or DI API)
features.write_requirement: SAP Service Layer or DI API (not available via direct SQL)

features.name: Production Order Tracking
features.description: Track production orders (OWOR) in SGA. Show which items are being produced, their status, and link to label printing once production is complete
features.impact: MEDIUM — 672 production orders exist. Links manufacturing to labeling workflow
implementation: Add get_production_orders(status=None, item_code=None) to sap_connector.py
New route /production/ with status filters (Planned/Released/Closed)
Show BOM components per production order (WOR1 lines)
When production order is Released, auto-suggest label generation for output item
Link production batch to OBTN for traceability

features.name: Customer-Specific Label Profiles
features.description: Some customers may require specific label formats or additional regulatory info. Link customer code from OCRD to label template preferences
features.impact: MEDIUM — Enables per-customer label customization automatically when loading SAP orders
implementation: Add customer_code field to label template selection
When loading SAP order, auto-select template by customer preferences
Store customer → template mapping in local config
Show customer info (name, address, RFC) in order detail

features.name: Inbound Receiving Labels (Purchase Orders)
features.description: When goods arrive (OPDN), auto-generate receiving labels with batch info, supplier name, and incoming inspection dates
features.impact: MEDIUM — 1,336 goods receipts exist. Labels for incoming material with supplier batch → internal batch mapping
implementation: Add get_goods_receipts(days_back=30) to sap_connector.py
New endpoint POST /labels/receiving/load to load a goods receipt
Generate receiving labels with: ItemCode, Supplier Batch, Internal Batch, MnfDate, ExpDate, Warehouse
Link to bin location assignment

phase_3_advanced.title: Phase 3 — Advanced Integration (1-2 months)
phase_3_advanced.description: Advanced features that require deeper SAP integration or business process changes

features.name: Bin Location Tracking
features.description: Integrate bin locations (OBIN) with SGA. When scanning a product, show its physical location in the warehouse and suggest bin placement
features.impact: HIGH for warehouse operations — 493 bin locations available. Enables pick/put-away optimization
implementation: Add get_bin_locations(warehouse=None) and get_item_bin(item_code) to sap_connector.py
Show bin location on label detail and in inventory dashboard
Add bin location QR code to GHS labels (optional template field)
Scan-to-locate: scan barcode → show bin location

features.name: Bill of Materials (BOM) Hazard Roll-up
features.description: For compound/mixed products (OITT/ITT1), automatically aggregate GHS hazard data from component items to generate the mixture label
features.impact: HIGH for regulatory compliance — 96 BOMs exist. Auto-calculates mixture hazard classification per GHS rules
implementation: Add get_bom(item_code) to sap_connector.py
For each BOM component, fetch GHS data (U_Peligro, U_Word, etc.)
Apply GHS mixture classification rules (additivity, cutoff concentrations)
Generate combined hazard profile for the finished product
Flag BOM products that need label review when components change
risks: GHS mixture classification rules are complex (requires Chapter 3 GHS logic)
May need chemist review for accuracy

features.name: Full Batch Traceability Chain
features.description: Complete traceability from purchase → receipt → production → label → delivery using IBT1 transaction lines. Answer: 'Which customer received batch X?'
features.impact: CRITICAL for recalls — Given a batch number, trace forward (who received it) and backward (where it came from)
implementation: Add trace_batch(item_code, batch_number, direction='forward') to sap_connector.py
Query IBT1 for all documents touching this batch (BaseType: 20=GR PO, 59=Goods Receipt, 60=Goods Issue, 15=Delivery, 202=Production)
Build traceability tree: Supplier → Receipt → Production → Label → Delivery → Customer
New route /traceability/<batch> with visual chain diagram
Recall assistant: select batch → show all affected customers and deliveries

features.name: SDS/CoA Document Attachment Linking
features.description: Link Safety Data Sheets (SDS) and Certificates of Analysis (CoA) from SAP attachments (ATC1) to products, making them accessible in the SGA web interface
features.impact: MEDIUM — 24,644 attachments exist in SAP. Direct access to SDS/CoA without opening SAP client
implementation: Add get_item_attachments(item_code) to sap_connector.py via OITM.AtcEntry → ATC1
Show attachment list on product detail page (filter for PDF/DOC)
Serve attachment files via SGA web (read from SAP file share path)
Optionally embed SDS link/QR code on GHS label
risks: File paths in ATC1 may reference SAP server file system — need network access to attachment folder

features.name: Stock Transfer Labels
features.description: When stock is transferred between warehouses (OWTR/WTR1), auto-generate transfer labels with source→destination warehouse, batch info, and bin location
features.impact: MEDIUM — 971 transfers recorded. Ensures batch labels are updated when items move between warehouses
implementation: Add get_stock_transfers(days_back=30) to sap_connector.py
New endpoint for loading transfer documents into label queue
Transfer label template: source warehouse → destination warehouse + batch

features.name: Expiry Date Watchdog & Alerts
features.description: Monitor batch expiry dates (OBTN.ExpDate) and alert when batches are approaching expiration. Suggest relabeling or quarantine actions
features.impact: HIGH for compliance — Proactive expiry management prevents shipping expired material
implementation: Scheduled job queries OBTN WHERE ExpDate < CURRENT_DATE + 30 days AND status=active
Dashboard widget showing batches expiring soon (30/60/90 days)
Email/notification alerts for critical expiry items
Link to re-inspection label generation
Batch status update (quarantine expired batches)

phase_4_write_back.title: Phase 4 — SAP Write-Back Features (requires SAP Service Layer)
phase_4_write_back.description: Features that require WRITING data back to SAP. These need SAP Service Layer or DI API access, which is NOT available via direct hdbcli SQL queries (read-only). These are listed for planning but cannot be implemented with current direct DB connection

features.name: Auto-Create Delivery Notes from SGA
features.description: When labels are printed and verified, automatically create a delivery note (ODLN) in SAP
features.impact: HIGH — Bridges the gap between label printing and shipping documentation
features.requirement: SAP Service Layer REST API or SAP DI API (COM/.NET). Cannot INSERT into SAP tables directly via SQL — SAP validation and posting logic would be bypassed
features.alternative: For now, show a 'Ready to Ship' status in SGA that warehouse supervisors use as a signal to create the delivery in SAP client

features.name: Write Batch Info from Label Back to SAP
features.description: When a new batch is assigned during labeling, write the batch number back to SAP OBTN or update batch attributes
features.requirement: SAP Service Layer or DI API
features.alternative: Export batch assignments as CSV report for SAP data import

features.name: Update Order Status from SGA
features.description: When all items in an order are labeled and ready, update the SAP order status or add a comment
features.requirement: SAP Service Layer or DI API
features.alternative: Use the existing local order_status tracking and sync SAP status read-only

features.name: Populate GHS UDFs in SAP from SGA
features.description: When GHS data is entered/updated in SGA's local database, push it back to SAP OITM UDFs (U_Peligro, U_Word, etc.)
features.requirement: SAP Service Layer or DI API
features.alternative: Export GHS data as structured file for SAP import via DTW (Data Transfer Workbench)

connection_improvements.title: Connection Pool / Singleton Pattern
connection_improvements.description: Current code reconnects on every endpoint. Implement a connection pool or singleton with auto-reconnect and health checks

connection_improvements.title: Refactor Raw SQL in labels.py
connection_improvements.description: The tare weight fallback query in labels.py uses raw SQL directly on OITM via cursor. Move this to a sap_connector.py method (get_item_weights) for consistency

connection_improvements.title: Add Authentication to Public Sync Endpoint
connection_improvements.description: POST /orders/api/public/sync triggers unauthenticated SAP queries — add API key or rate limiting to prevent abuse

connection_improvements.title: SAP Query Caching Layer
connection_improvements.description: Add Redis or in-memory TTL cache for frequently accessed SAP data (product master, stock levels) to reduce database load. Cache 5-15 minutes for stock, 1 hour for product master

connection_improvements.title: SAP Service Layer Evaluation
connection_improvements.description: Evaluate setting up SAP Business One Service Layer (REST API) on the SAP server for Phase 4 write-back features. Service Layer runs on the same server and provides safe document creation with full SAP validation

data_quality.title: Populate U_TS_NumCAS and U_TS_NomQuim in SAP
data_quality.description: CAS Number and Chemical Name UDFs are 0% populated in SAP. SGA's local CSV has this data. Plan a bulk upload via SAP DTW to populate these fields

data_quality.title: Standardize Pictogram Code Mapping
data_quality.description: SAP uses single-letter codes (G, H, I, E) for pictograms while SGA uses full names (flame, health_hazard, etc.). Create and maintain a mapping table

data_quality.title: Batch Date Quality Review
data_quality.description: Some OBTN rows have MnfDate in the future (2027-2029) which appear to be data entry errors. Add validation when displaying batch dates in SGA
data_quality.example: VAR-QB00056 batch DAV05/25 has MnfDate=2029-07-04 which is likely incorrect

recommended_execution_order.name: Refactor raw SQL to sap_connector
recommended_execution_order.why: Clean architecture before adding more queries

recommended_execution_order.name: Batch stock picker from SAP
recommended_execution_order.why: Eliminates batch guessing, operators love this

recommended_execution_order.name: GHS data from SAP UDFs
recommended_execution_order.why: Single source of truth for GHS data

recommended_execution_order.name: Product master sync
recommended_execution_order.why: Keeps product list in sync with SAP

recommended_execution_order.name: Expiry date watchdog
recommended_execution_order.why: Compliance critical, uses existing OBTN data
